title: Reiquarium Images New Interpretations and Ideas
display_title: Reiquarium Images New Interpretations and Ideas
wikitext:
[]: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by Menage » Sun Jun 23, 2019 6:30 pm I'm rather new here and don't know if this is the right place to post this, but here I go. I'm referring to these images: https://wiki.evageeks.org/Theory_and_Analysis:Dummy_Plug_Plant_Images Read up on them if you're unfamiliar. I'm fascinated by them and have always wondered what the 'unknowable' ones could be depicting, so I gave it a shot. I don't know if some of these connections have been made before or if they've been solved years ago. First comparison SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 24, 2019 1:44 am The "First Impact" connection is intriguing, and your juxtaposition of choice makes it all the more so. No one has suggested this before, to my knowledge, so thank you for the valuable contribution! If I have any misgivings, they come from two things: (A) It's a bit inconclusive, but the photo always gave me the impression of two linked objects in some sort of fluidic medium. Can all of that wavy stuff be reasonably interpreted as a "spacescape", as it were? (B) The image is inescapably distorted, but, at least in this edit (which I created by basically just cobbling together sections from multiple frames that showed detail instead of being blurry), we have one body that is very clearly round, and the other is... rather less so. The one on the right seems to have a more oval-shaped profile, as far as I can tell. Maybe this is still just an artifact, but it's something to consider.

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by Menage » Mon Jun 24, 2019 3:41 am Regarding the shape: I don't think it's distinctly oval enough to rule out a distorted planetoid. Even in the image I provided Earth looks somewhat oval. I know it doesn't quite look like the spheres are touching, but if you wanted to you could interpret some of the surrounding specs as debris that is being propelled away from the impact point.

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Fri Jun 28, 2019 8:31 am It's interesting because the theory regarding the origin of the moon is actually irl called the Giant Impact Theory. So naturally, that would be First Impact in NGE when Lilith arrived and messed up everything Adam was doing...which wasn't much seeing as she fell on her own spear. And given Rei's association with the moon and moon imagery throughout the show, that easily adds up. But yeah, nice catch with the Magi image! Sure it's just interpretation at this point, but that looks pretty damn close. As far as the third image...Is that not part of Adam's Geofront? The "pool" area could even be the White Moon's version of the lake we see surrounding Never HQ in the Black Moon... It could be a crater of some kind. That thing in the middle the mechanical looking "arm" looks like some sort of machinery that gets pretty far down...Perhaps here they were excavating Adam from the ice? I guess its hard to tell if that's just really big hole or water.... Either way, this is some rather refreshing analysis and your speculations are very unique and interesting. Especially considering we've been sitting on these images for years without the slightest ability to make heads or tails of most of them!

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by Menage » Fri Jun 28, 2019 11:49 am Well I'm glad this is being appreciated I actually thought about the 3rd image being inside/on top of the White Moon, but what threw me off was that thing in the bottom-right corner. It looks out of place in relation to something the size of Nerv HQ. It makes the whole "pool" look weirdly small. Or is it just that far in the foreground? Doesn't this look like a tiled floor with a thing screwed on top?

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jun 28, 2019 12:09 pm Bottom right corner looks like it's a ledge overlooking the scene. The tiling brings to mind the distinct plates that are visible in the Geofront ceiling. We never see those from the other side, do we...? The perfectly round aperture that's left on top of the Black Moon after the city has been blown completely away makes it seem like there's a portal built into the top; and the construction of Tokyo-3 simply took advantage of this preexisting feature, same as it used the excavated area at the very top of the Moon. So could we be seeing here what the original seal on one of the Moon's portals looked like? Fascinating stuff!

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Fri Jun 28, 2019 12:45 pm Menage wrote: Doesn't this look like a tiled floor with a thing screwed on top?

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Fri Jun 28, 2019 12:57 pm Reichu wrote: So could we be seeing here what the original seal on one of the Moon's portals looked like? Fascinating stuff!

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by Menage » Fri Jun 28, 2019 1:11 pm The Geofront/Nerv HQ is the open space inside of the moon or at least a part of it...

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Fri Jun 28, 2019 1:17 pm Menage wrote: This is the best visualization I know of: https://sketchfab.com/3d-models/evangelion-geofront-1fb54331408147c3b9fb502b6c883cb0

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by Menage » Fri Jun 28, 2019 1:55 pm Only that we have no real way of knowing if the hole or the cap have always been there, or if it's just entirely man-made. I mean they do talk about having 22 layers of armor plates in there, so I doubt the whole lid is an original feature.

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Fri Jun 28, 2019 2:26 pm Menage wrote: Only that we have no real way of knowing if the hole or the cap have always been there, or if it's just entirely man-made. I mean they do talk about having 22 layers of armor plates in there, so I doubt the whole lid is an original feature.

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by Menage » Fri Jun 28, 2019 2:51 pm There's some good discussion about the Eva launch routes here: https://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:Supplemental_(Elevators_and_Eva_Lifts)

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by robersora » Sat Jun 29, 2019 8:27 am Damn, the Geofront Diorama is so cool!!

[]: Re: Reiquarium Images - New Interpretations and Ideas             Quote Post by Onii-chan » Tue Oct 27, 2020 3:19 am Damn it just occurred to me looking at this image: That the Geofront is almost like a giant eyeball. The excavated area is like the iris and the hole at the top where the city sits is the pupil.
